latency_ms.note: informational; latency is NOT a pass/fail gate

results.question: How many organizations are there?
results.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM organizations;

results.question: List all plan names.
results.generated_sql: SELECT name FROM plans LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many users have the role 'admin'?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM users 
WHERE role = 'admin'

results.question: Which organizations are inactive?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT id, name, plan_id, created_at, country, is_active
FROM organizations
WHERE is_active = false
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many invoices are in 'open' status?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM invoices 
WHERE status = 'open'

results.question: What are the distinct subscription statuses?
results.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT status FROM subscriptions;

results.question: How many users are there in total?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_users
FROM users

results.question: List the plan tiers and their monthly prices.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT tier, monthly_price
FROM plans
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many payments were made by the 'card' method?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM payments 
WHERE method = 'card'

results.question: Which countries do organizations operate in?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT country
FROM organizations
WHERE country IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY country
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many plans are there?
results.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM plans

results.question: List the email addresses of all users with the 'viewer' role.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT email
FROM users
WHERE role = 'viewer'
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many subscriptions are there in total?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_subscriptions
FROM subscriptions

results.question: How many users belong to each organization?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(u.id) AS user_count
FROM organizations o
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY user_count DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: What is the total invoice amount per organization?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoices i
JOIN organizations o ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many organizations are on each plan tier?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.tier, COUNT(o.id) AS organization_count
FROM organizations o
LEFT JOIN plans p ON o.plan_id = p.id
WHERE o.is_active = true
GROUP BY p.tier
ORDER BY organization_count DESC
LIMIT 1000
results.reason: no consistent column mapping makes the rows match

results.question: What is the total revenue from paid invoices?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_revenue
FROM invoices
WHERE status = 'paid'

results.question: How many active subscriptions are there per plan?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.name, COUNT(s.id) AS active_subscriptions
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.id
WHERE s.status = 'active'
GROUP BY p.name
ORDER BY active_subscriptions DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: List each organization and its plan tier.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, p.tier
FROM organizations o
LEFT JOIN plans p ON o.plan_id = p.id
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many usage events did each event_type generate?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT event_type, COUNT(*) AS event_count
FROM usage_events
GROUP BY event_type
ORDER BY event_count DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: What is the average invoice amount per status?
How many users per role?
Total payments amount per payment method.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT method, SUM(amount) AS total_amount
FROM payments
GROUP BY method
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which organizations have an active subscription?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name
FROM organizations o
INNER JOIN subscriptions s ON s.organization_id = o.id
WHERE s.status = 'active'
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Count of canceled subscriptions.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status = 'canceled'

results.question: What is the total quantity of usage events per event type?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT event_type, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM usage_events
GROUP BY event_type
ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many invoices does each organization have?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(i.id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoices i
JOIN organizations o ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: What is the total number of seats across active subscriptions?
How many users were created in 2024?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM users 
WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01' 
  AND created_at < '2025-01-01';

results.question: List each plan name and its seat limit.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT name, seat_limit
FROM plans
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which organization has the most users?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(u.id) AS user_count
FROM organizations o
JOIN users u ON u.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY user_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Top 5 organizations by total paid invoice amount.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_paid_amount
FROM organizations o
JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id
WHERE i.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY total_paid_amount DESC
LIMIT 5

results.question: Which organizations have no usage events?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name
FROM organizations o
LEFT JOIN usage_events ue ON ue.organization_id = o.id
WHERE ue.id IS NULL
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Total payment amount per organization (via invoices).
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, SUM(p.amount) AS total_payment_amount
FROM payments p
JOIN invoices i ON p.invoice_id = i.id
JOIN organizations o ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY total_payment_amount DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Monthly count of usage events in 2024.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', occurred_at) AS month, COUNT(*) AS event_count
FROM usage_events
WHERE occurred_at >= '2024-01-01' AND occurred_at < '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', occurred_at)
ORDER BY month;

results.question: Which enterprise-tier organizations have more than 5 users?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name, COUNT(u.id) AS user_count
FROM organizations o
JOIN plans p ON o.plan_id = p.id
JOIN users u ON u.organization_id = o.id
WHERE p.tier = 'enterprise'
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
HAVING COUNT(u.id) > 5
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Organizations with open invoices but no payments.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name, i.id AS invoice_id, i.amount, i.issued_at, i.due_at
FROM organizations o
JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id
LEFT JOIN payments p ON p.invoice_id = i.id
WHERE i.status = 'open'
  AND p.id IS NULL
ORDER BY o.id, i.id
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Average number of seats on active subscriptions per plan tier.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.tier, AVG(s.seats) AS avg_seats
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.id
WHERE s.status = 'active'
GROUP BY p.tier
ORDER BY avg_seats DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which users have never been active (no last_active_at)?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT id, organization_id, email, full_name, role, created_at, last_active_at
FROM users
WHERE last_active_at IS NULL
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Total revenue (paid invoices) per plan tier.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.tier, SUM(i.amount) AS total_revenue
FROM invoices i
JOIN organizations o ON i.organization_id = o.id
JOIN plans p ON o.plan_id = p.id
WHERE i.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY p.tier
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Top 3 organizations by usage event count.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(ue.id) AS event_count
FROM organizations o
JOIN usage_events ue ON ue.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY event_count DESC
LIMIT 3

results.question: Organizations whose total invoiced amount exceeds 1000.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_invoiced
FROM organizations o
JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 1000
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Count of users created in each month of 2024.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month,
    COUNT(*) AS user_count
FROM users
WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND created_at < '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at)
ORDER BY month;

results.question: Which plans have no organizations assigned?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.id, p.name, p.tier, p.monthly_price, p.seat_limit
FROM plans p
LEFT JOIN organizations o ON o.plan_id = p.id
WHERE o.id IS NULL
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Among organizations that have generated usage events, how many distinct event types has each used?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(DISTINCT ue.event_type) AS distinct_event_types
FROM organizations o
INNER JOIN usage_events ue ON o.id = ue.organization_id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY distinct_event_types DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which organizations have more than 3 invoices?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(i.id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoices i
JOIN organizations o ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
HAVING COUNT(i.id) > 3
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Top 2 organizations by number of users.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(u.id) AS user_count
FROM organizations o
JOIN users u ON u.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY user_count DESC
LIMIT 2;

results.question: Total amount paid per organization, for organizations that have made payments.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, SUM(p.amount) AS total_amount_paid
FROM payments p
JOIN invoices i ON p.invoice_id = i.id
JOIN organizations o ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY total_amount_paid DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: How many distinct organizations have usage events?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT organization_id) AS distinct_organizations
FROM usage_events

results.question: For each plan tier, the number of distinct organizations.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.tier, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS organization_count
FROM organizations o
LEFT JOIN plans p ON o.plan_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.tier
ORDER BY organization_count DESC
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which users belong to organizations on the 'enterprise' tier?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT u.id, u.email, u.full_name, u.role, o.name AS organization_name
FROM users u
JOIN organizations o ON u.organization_id = o.id
JOIN plans p ON o.plan_id = p.id
WHERE p.tier = 'enterprise'
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Monthly total of paid invoice amounts in 2024.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', issued_at) AS month, SUM(amount) AS total_amount
FROM invoices
WHERE status = 'paid' AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM issued_at) = 2024
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', issued_at)
ORDER BY month
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Organizations with total paid revenue greater than 500.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name, SUM(i.amount) AS total_paid_revenue
FROM organizations o
JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id
WHERE i.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
HAVING SUM(i.amount) > 500
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Average payment amount per payment method.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT method, AVG(amount) AS average_amount
FROM payments
GROUP BY method
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which organizations have users but no usage events?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.id, o.name
FROM organizations o
INNER JOIN users u ON u.organization_id = o.id
LEFT JOIN usage_events ue ON ue.organization_id = o.id
WHERE ue.id IS NULL
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
LIMIT 1000

results.question: For each event type, the average quantity per event.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT event_type, AVG(quantity) AS avg_quantity
FROM usage_events
GROUP BY event_type
LIMIT 1000

results.question: Which organizations have more users than invoices?
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.name, COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) AS user_count, COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) AS invoice_count
FROM organizations o
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.organization_id = o.id
LEFT JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) > COUNT(DISTINCT i.id)
ORDER BY user_count DESC
LIMIT 1000
